questions.prompt: Which Claude tier is the right default for most production traffic?

choices.text: Sonnet. Strong general intelligence at a fraction of Opus cost. Move to Haiku for high-volume simple tasks, move to Opus for the hardest reasoning — but Sonnet is the right starting point.

questions.explanation: Sonnet hits the sweet spot of intelligence vs. cost vs. speed for most production AI products. Always-Opus is wasteful; always-Haiku misses nuance. Start with Sonnet, then route easy traffic down and hard traffic up.

questions.prompt: You're building a chatbot with mixed traffic — most questions are simple, ~10% involve multi-step reasoning. What's the right pattern?

choices.text: Add a small Haiku-powered router that classifies questions as easy/hard. Easy ones go to Haiku or Sonnet; hard ones go to Opus. Routing costs cents per thousand, savings are dollars per thousand.
Send everything to Haiku and hope.

questions.explanation: Mixed-tier routing is the highest-leverage cost optimization in Claude production work. A tiny classifier upfront picks the right model for each turn. Cuts bills 60–80% vs. always-Opus with no measurable quality drop on the easy traffic.
